userStories.title: Initialize SQLite database schema
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a persistent local database so that session data survives between terminal sessions.
acceptanceCriteria: A devtimer.db SQLite file is created in ~/.devtimer/ on first use
projects table exists with columns: id (pk), name (text), path (text unique), created_at (datetime)
sessions table exists with columns: id (pk), project_id (fk), started_at (datetime), ended_at (datetime nullable), notes (text default '')
Schema is created idempotently — running init twice does not error or duplicate tables

userStories.title: Implement git repo auto-detection
userStories.description: As a user, I want the tool to automatically know which project I'm in so I never have to name it manually.
acceptanceCriteria: A detect_repo() function walks up the directory tree from cwd until it finds a .git directory or hits the filesystem root
Returns the repo root path and infers the project name from the root directory's basename
Returns null (not an error) when called outside any git repo
Covered by at least two unit tests: one inside a repo, one outside

userStories.title: Add project auto-registration on first session
userStories.description: As a user, I want new repos to be registered automatically the first time I track them so I don't need a separate setup command.
acceptanceCriteria: A get_or_create_project(path, name) function inserts a project row if one with that path does not exist, or returns the existing one
Returned object includes id, name, path
If called outside a git repo, raises a clear error: 'Not inside a git repository'
